title: [AggressiveInstCombine] POPCNT generation for bit-count pattern
author: rohitaggarwal007
description: The proposal is to enhance LLVM by teaching it to recognize the pattern and replace it with the hardware POPCNT instruction.

tests.test_body:
define dso_local noundef i64 @popcnt1_64(i64 noundef %uWord) local_unnamed_addr {
entry:
  %and = and i64 %uWord, 6148914691236517205
  %shr = lshr i64 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i64 %shr, 6148914691236517205
  %add = add nuw i64 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i64 %add, 3689348814741910323
  %shr3 = lshr i64 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i64 %shr3, 3689348814741910323
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i64 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i64 %add5, 1085102592571150095
  %shr7 = lshr i64 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i64 %shr7, 1085102592571150095
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i64 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i64 %add9, 71777214294589695
  %shr11 = lshr i64 %add9, 8
  %and12 = and i64 %shr11, 71777214294589695
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i64 %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and i64 %add13, 281470681808895
  %shr15 = lshr i64 %add13, 16
  %and16 = and i64 %shr15, 281470681808895
  %add17 = add nuw nsw i64 %and16, %and14
  %and18 = and i64 %add17, 4294967295
  %shr19 = lshr i64 %add17, 32
  %add20 = add nuw nsw i64 %and18, %shr19
  ret i64 %add20
}

define dso_local noundef range(i32 0, 59) i32 @popcnt1_32(i32 noundef %uWord) local_unnamed_addr {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %uWord, 1431655765
  %shr = lshr i32 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i32 %shr, 1431655765
  %add = add nuw i32 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i32 %add, 858993459
  %shr3 = lshr i32 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i32 %shr3, 858993459
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i32 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i32 %add5, 252645135
  %shr7 = lshr i32 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i32 %shr7, 252645135
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i32 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i32 %add9, 16711935
  %shr11 = lshr i32 %add9, 8
  %and12 = and i32 %shr11, 16711935
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i32 %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and i32 %add13, 65535
  %shr15 = lshr i32 %add13, 16
  %add16 = add nuw nsw i32 %and14, %shr15
  ret i32 %add16
}

define i16 @popcnt1_16_variant2(i16 noundef %uWord) {
  %and = and i16 %uWord, 21845
  %shr = lshr i16 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i16 %shr, 21845
  %add = add nuw i16 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i16 %add, 13107
  %shr3 = lshr i16 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i16 %shr3, 13107
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i16 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i16 %add5, 3855
  %shr7 = lshr i16 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i16 %shr7, 3855
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i16 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i16 %add9, 255
  %shr11 = lshr i16 %add9, 8
  %and12 = and i16 %shr11, 255
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i16 %and10, %and12
  ret i16 %add13
}

define i16 @popcnt1_16(i16 noundef %uWord) {
  %and = and i16 %uWord, 21845
  %shr = lshr i16 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i16 %shr, 21845
  %add = add nuw i16 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i16 %add, 13107
  %shr3 = lshr i16 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i16 %shr3, 13107
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i16 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i16 %add5, 3855
  %shr7 = lshr i16 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i16 %shr7, 3855
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i16 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i16 %add9, 255
  %shr11 = lshr i16 %add9, 8
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i16 %shr11, %and10
  ret i16 %add13
}

define <8 x i16> @popcnt1_16vec(<8 x i16> %uWord) {
  %and = and <8 x i16> %uWord, splat (i16 21845)
  %shr = lshr <8 x i16> %uWord, splat (i16 1)
  %and1 = and <8 x i16> %shr, splat (i16 21845)
  %add = add nuw <8 x i16> %and1, %and
  %and2 = and <8 x i16> %add, splat (i16 13107)
  %shr3 = lshr <8 x i16> %add, splat (i16 2)
  %and4 = and <8 x i16> %shr3, splat (i16 13107)
  %add5 = add nuw nsw <8 x i16> %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and <8 x i16> %add5, splat (i16 3855)
  %shr7 = lshr <8 x i16> %add5, splat (i16 4)
  %and8 = and <8 x i16> %shr7, splat (i16 3855)
  %add9 = add nuw nsw <8 x i16> %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and <8 x i16> %add9, splat (i16 255)
  %shr11 = lshr <8 x i16> %add9, splat (i16 8)
  %add13 = add nuw nsw <8 x i16> %shr11, %and10
  ret <8 x i16> %add13
}

define <4 x i32> @popcnt1_32vec(<4 x i32> %uWord) {
  %and = and <4 x i32> %uWord, splat (i32 1431655765)
  %shr = lshr <4 x i32> %uWord, splat (i32 1)
  %and1 = and <4 x i32> %shr, splat (i32 1431655765)
  %add = add nuw <4 x i32> %and1, %and
  %and2 = and <4 x i32> %add, splat (i32 858993459)
  %shr3 = lshr <4 x i32> %add, splat (i32 2)
  %and4 = and <4 x i32> %shr3, splat (i32 858993459)
  %add5 = add nuw nsw <4 x i32> %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and <4 x i32> %add5, splat (i32 252645135)
  %shr7 = lshr <4 x i32> %add5, splat (i32 4)
  %and8 = and <4 x i32> %shr7, splat (i32 252645135)
  %add9 = add nuw nsw <4 x i32> %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and <4 x i32> %add9, splat (i32 16711935)
  %shr11 = lshr <4 x i32> %add9, splat (i32 8)
  %and12 = and <4 x i32> %shr11, splat (i32 16711935)
  %add13 = add nuw nsw <4 x i32> %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and <4 x i32> %add13, splat (i32 65535)
  %shr15 = lshr <4 x i32> %add13, splat (i32 16)
  %add16 = add nuw nsw <4 x i32> %and14, %shr15
  ret <4 x i32> %add16
}

define <2 x i64> @popcnt1_64vec(<2 x i64> %uWord) {
  %and = and <2 x i64> %uWord, splat (i64 6148914691236517205)
  %shr = lshr <2 x i64> %uWord, splat (i64 1)
  %and1 = and <2 x i64> %shr, splat (i64 6148914691236517205)
  %add = add nuw <2 x i64> %and1, %and
  %and2 = and <2 x i64> %add, splat (i64 3689348814741910323)
  %shr3 = lshr <2 x i64> %add, splat (i64 2)
  %and4 = and <2 x i64> %shr3, splat (i64 3689348814741910323)
  %add5 = add nuw nsw <2 x i64> %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and <2 x i64> %add5, splat (i64 1085102592571150095)
  %shr7 = lshr <2 x i64> %add5, splat (i64 4)
  %and8 = and <2 x i64> %shr7, splat (i64 1085102592571150095)
  %add9 = add nuw nsw <2 x i64> %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and <2 x i64> %add9, splat (i64 71777214294589695)
  %shr11 = lshr <2 x i64> %add9, splat (i64 8)
  %and12 = and <2 x i64> %shr11, splat (i64 71777214294589695)
  %add13 = add nuw nsw <2 x i64> %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and <2 x i64> %add13, splat (i64 281470681808895)
  %shr15 = lshr <2 x i64> %add13, splat (i64 16)
  %and16 = and <2 x i64> %shr15, splat (i64 281470681808895)
  %add17 = add nuw nsw <2 x i64> %and16, %and14
  %and18 = and <2 x i64> %add17, splat (i64 4294967295)
  %shr19 = lshr <2 x i64> %add17, splat (i64 32)
  %add20 = add nuw nsw <2 x i64> %and18, %shr19
  ret <2 x i64> %add20
}

define dso_local noundef range(i32 0, 59) i32 @popcnt1_32_variant2(i32 noundef %uWord) local_unnamed_addr {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %uWord, 1431655765
  %shr = lshr i32 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i32 %shr, 1431655765
  %add = add nuw i32 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i32 %add, 858993459
  %shr3 = lshr i32 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i32 %shr3, 858993459
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i32 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i32 %add5, 252645135
  %shr7 = lshr i32 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i32 %shr7, 252645135
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i32 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i32 %add9, 16711935
  %shr11 = lshr i32 %add9, 8
  %and12 = and i32 %shr11, 16711935
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i32 %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and i32 %add13, 65535
  %shr15 = lshr i32 %add13, 16
  %and16 = and i32 %shr15, 65535
  %add17 = add nuw nsw i32 %and14, %and16
  ret i32 %add17
}

define i64 @popcnt1_64_variant2(i64 noundef %uWord) {
  %and = and i64 %uWord, 6148914691236517205
  %shr = lshr i64 %uWord, 1
  %and1 = and i64 %shr, 6148914691236517205
  %add = add nuw i64 %and1, %and
  %and2 = and i64 %add, 3689348814741910323
  %shr3 = lshr i64 %add, 2
  %and4 = and i64 %shr3, 3689348814741910323
  %add5 = add nuw nsw i64 %and4, %and2
  %and6 = and i64 %add5, 1085102592571150095
  %shr7 = lshr i64 %add5, 4
  %and8 = and i64 %shr7, 1085102592571150095
  %add9 = add nuw nsw i64 %and8, %and6
  %and10 = and i64 %add9, 71777214294589695
  %shr11 = lshr i64 %add9, 8
  %and12 = and i64 %shr11, 71777214294589695
  %add13 = add nuw nsw i64 %and12, %and10
  %and14 = and i64 %add13, 281470681808895
  %shr15 = lshr i64 %add13, 16
  %and16 = and i64 %shr15, 281470681808895
  %add17 = add nuw nsw i64 %and16, %and14
  %and18 = and i64 %add17, 4294967295
  %shr19 = lshr i64 %add17, 32
  %and20 = and i64 %shr19, 4294967295
  %add21 = add nuw nsw i64 %and18, %and20
  ret i64 %add21
}

comments.author: rohitaggarwal007
comments.body:
> > As there are matches on llvm-opt-benchmark we can consider this one. This recognizes two different patterns, which should be split into different PRs.
> 
> Sure, I will split the PR into two PRs
> 
> > This needs more test coverage. Even ignoring negative tests, the implementations claim to handle 16, 32, 64 and 128 bit integers, but there's no test coverage for that, and I doubt these are actually handled correctly based on a cursory look at the code (lots of hardcoded constants for 32-bit values).
> 
> Sure, I will do the needful.

comments.author: rohitaggarwal007
comments.body:
> As there are matches on llvm-opt-benchmark we can consider this one. This recognizes two different patterns, which should be split into different PRs.
> 
> This needs more test coverage. Even ignoring negative tests, the implementations claim to handle 16, 32, 64 and 128 bit integers, but there's no test coverage for that, and I doubt these are actually handled correctly based on a cursory look at the code (lots of hardcoded constants for 32-bit values).

I have added more test cases

comments.author: rohitaggarwal007
comments.body:
> There are still no negative tests?

Sure, Will add more test case
